title: Day 1 · Hello Agent
task: Use the echo tool to say hi

card.label: Use the echo tool to say hi

ledger.delta: messages += user('Use the echo tool to say hi')

frames.caption: CLI parses the input, wraps it as a user message, and drops it into messages. This is the first thing the model sees.

messages.content: Use the echo tool to say hi

frames.caption: MockProvider sees the user message, recognizes the tool intent, and returns tool_calls=[echo]. The model only proposes — it never executes.

messages.content: Use the echo tool to say hi

frames.caption: harness looks up echo in the ToolRegistry. Day 1 has no permission layer yet — any registered tool passes. This gate gets stricter every following day.

messages.content: Use the echo tool to say hi

frames.caption: harness invokes the Python function and gets 'hi' back. WORLD is just a local function today; from Day 3 it becomes files, from Day 5 it becomes the shell.

messages.content: Use the echo tool to say hi

frames.caption: harness writes the observation back to messages, role='tool', tool_call_id matching the model's proposal. This is the world → model return path.

messages.content: Use the echo tool to say hi

frames.caption: MockProvider receives messages a second time, sees the latest tool result, and returns plain text with stop_reason=end_turn. The Agent Loop exits.

messages.content: Use the echo tool to say hi

frames.caption: CLI prints the model's final text to the terminal. The whole trace (user → tool_use → tool_result → final) lives in messages — saveable, replayable.

messages.content: Use the echo tool to say hi
